## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Hidden git-backed checkpoints are created before mutating agent tools and can be inspected/restored without creating a workspace `.git`.

Real environment tested: Blacksmith Testbox through Crabbox on Linux, plus local macOS focused tests.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: In a temp workspace, build OpenClaw, create a checkpoint, mutate a tracked file, mutate a gitignored non-excluded file, mutate an excluded `.env`, add a new file, replace `src/` with a file, diff the checkpoint, targeted-restore `src/app.ts`, then full-restore the original checkpoint hash with `--yes`.

Evidence after fix: Remote diff showed `note.txt`, `ignored.txt`, `new.txt`, and the file/directory conflict; targeted restore recreated `src/app.ts`; full restore reverted `note.txt` and `ignored.txt`, removed `new.txt`, preserved changed `.env`, kept the workspace free of `.git`, and listed both pre-restore checkpoints plus the original seed checkpoint.

Observed result after fix: Workspace returned to the original checkpointed state for included files while excluded secret-like state remained untouched; checkpoint history remained available for pre-restore recovery.

What was not tested: A full live model turn invoking actual write/edit/exec through a provider; covered at the tool wrapper, CLI, manager, and remote end-to-end simulation layers.

target.title: fix(auth): clean refresh contention diagnostics
target.body:
## Summary
- Keep OAuth `refresh_contention` diagnostics deterministic by avoiding nested file-lock cause flattening in user-facing refresh failures.
- Preserve programmatic `code` / `lockPath` on `OAuthManagerRefreshError` while redacting lock paths from messages and JSON-facing diagnostics, including structured non-`Error` causes.
- Add regression coverage for manager-level redaction and resolver-level single-message surfacing.

## Related PRs and Issues
- Follow-up to https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/82670, which landed the first auth refresh redaction/pass over the agents reliability slice.
- Related to https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/67876 and closed https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/26322, the original cross-agent OAuth refresh serialization/race fix.
- Adjacent to https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/82117 and https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/80738 for Codex OAuth refresh fallback/forced-refresh behavior.
- Adjacent to https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/83312 for legacy Codex OAuth sidecar compatibility.
- Related but not closing https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/42541; that issue is already closed by the prior auth-refresh work.
- Closes: none. Supersedes: none.

## Verification
- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/auth-profiles/oauth-manager.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/oauth.test.ts`: 4 files / 76 tests passed.
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/agents/auth-profiles/oauth-manager.ts`: 0 warnings / 0 errors.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch` on head `d754e783bc69e4c50dd57d79bd9db31519540ea5`: no accepted/actionable findings.
- `pnpm check:changed` through Blacksmith Testbox via `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider blacksmith-testbox ...`: `tbx_01krwxe431qkbdqzyvhcbgggdq`, GitHub Actions https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26018020246, exit 0. The changed gate widened to all lanes because the rebased branch triggered a 546-file sync/change threshold, so this is broader than the five-file PR diff.
- PR head CI sanity: Real behavior proof succeeded on https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26018018362; merge state is clean.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: OAuth refresh contention failures no longer expose file-lock paths in user-facing refresh diagnostics, and the contention text is surfaced once instead of duplicated through cause-chain flattening.

Real environment tested: Fresh OpenClaw gwt branch off current `origin/main`, with focused local auth-profile tests, focused lint, branch re-review, and Blacksmith Testbox changed-gate proof on the pushed PR head.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/auth-profiles/oauth-manager.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/oauth.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/agents/auth-profiles/oauth-manager.ts`; `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch`; `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider blacksmith-testbox --blacksmith-org openclaw --blacksmith-workflow .github/workflows/ci-check-testbox.yml --blacksmith-job check --blacksmith-ref main --idle-timeout 90m --ttl 240m --timing-json -- CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_PARALLEL=6 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 OPENCLAW_TESTBOX_REMOTE_RUN=1 pnpm check:changed`.

Evidence after fix: Regression tests assert `refresh_contention` messages do not contain the synthetic lock path or `file lock timeout`, including structured non-`Error` causes, and that both `OAuth token refresh failed` and `OAuth refresh failed (refresh_contention)` appear only once. Autoreview found no actionable correctness issues, and Testbox-through-Crabbox passed `pnpm check:changed` with exit 0.

Observed result after fix: Focused auth-profile tests passed, focused oxlint passed, branch review passed, and Testbox changed-gate passed on the pushed head `d754e783bc69e4c50dd57d79bd9db31519540ea5`.

What was not tested: No live OAuth provider refresh was forced; this is a deterministic diagnostic/redaction hardening path covered by mocked contention errors and manager-level error construction.

target.title: feat(browser): add browserbase profile driver with per-attach connectUrl resolution
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a new `browserbase` profile driver to the OpenClaw browser plugin. Profiles using this driver declare a Browserbase keep-alive session id and an env var name for the API key; on every CDP attach OpenClaw fetches a fresh `connectUrl` from `GET /v1/sessions/<id>` and hands it to the existing CDP pipeline. Eliminates the stale-signing-key fragility that breaks stateful Browserbase work today.

```jsonc
"browserbase-lexzur": {
  "driver": "browserbase",
  "browserbaseSessionId": "44589bac-33f0-4080-9eff-f1b3e3a0bf9c",
  "browserbaseApiKeyEnv": "BROWSERBASE_API_KEY",
  "attachOnly": true
}
```

## Why

The existing `cdpUrl: "wss://...?signingKey=..."` model puts a short-lived JWT into static config. Browserbase rotates the signing key well before the session itself expires — verified empirically with two consecutive `GET /v1/sessions/<id>` calls returning different `signingKey` values for the same session. Once the embedded key ages out, every CDP attach gets `1008 unauthorized` and any multi-step stateful workflow (login → multi-step → submit) breaks mid-flow.

The fix is the documented Browserbase pattern: fetch a fresh `connectUrl` via the Sessions API on each attach. Bearer auth comes from the operator's own credentials, refreshed on each request, no embedded lifetime to track.

## What changed

### New module — `extensions/browser/src/browser/browserbase-session.ts`

- `fetchBrowserbaseConnectUrl(sessionId, apiKeyEnv, opts)` — issues `GET /v1/sessions/<id>` with `X-BB-API-Key`. 8s default timeout, combines internal AbortController with caller-supplied `signal`. Three named error classes: `BrowserbaseAuthConfigError`, `BrowserbaseSessionUnavailableError`, `BrowserbaseSessionMalformedError`.
- `withResolvedCdpUrl(profile, opts)` — identity for non-browserbase drivers (same reference returned, zero allocations, no fetch); shallow-copy with freshly resolved `cdpUrl` / `cdpHost` / `cdpIsLoopback: false` for browserbase drivers.
- **No caching.** Two calls = two HTTP requests by design — the whole point is signing-key rotation.

### Resolver branch — `extensions/browser/src/browser/config.ts`

- `resolveProfile()` gains a `browserbase` branch that validates `browserbaseSessionId` is UUID-shaped and `browserbaseApiKeyEnv` matches `/^[A-Z][A-Z0-9_]*$/`. Returns `cdpUrl: ""` — URL is resolved lazily at attach time.
- Operator-friendly fail-fast at config load (not on first hot-path attach) for missing/malformed fields.

### Capability branch — `extensions/browser/src/browser/profile-capabilities.ts`

- Third branch for `driver === "browserbase"` → `mode: "remote-cdp"`, `isRemote: true`, `usesPersistentPlaywright: true`, all `/json/*` endpoint flags off (Browserbase doesn't expose JSON tab endpoints; everything goes through the signed WS connectUrl).

### Wired into CDP attach call sites

- `server-context.tab-ops.ts` — `readTabs` + `openTab` Playwright legs.
- `server-context.selection.ts` — `focusTab` + `closeTab` Playwright legs.
- `server-context.availability.ts` — `isReachable` / `isHttpReachable` probe via cheap `fetchBrowserbaseConnectUrl` for browserbase profiles (instead of full CDP WS attach per status poll).

### Default-timeout bump (separate commit `700639f1df`)

Based on real-world transcontinental remote-CDP topology (SFO-hosted Browserbase + Linux gateway). Previous 1500/2000 defaults were too aggressive — left zero margin on healthy attaches under transit jitter.

- `remoteCdpTimeoutMs`: 1500 → 3000
- `remoteCdpHandshakeTimeoutMs` floor: `max(remoteCdpTimeoutMs*2, 5000)` → resolves to 6000 by default. Preserves the `handshake ≥ 2× http` invariant for operators who customize one without bumping the other.

### Docs (separate commit `68f68af6f7`)

New subsection in `docs/tools/browser.md` covering keep-alive sessions, auto-create vs session-id distinction, the `POST /v1/sessions` curl example with `keepAlive: true`, the new profile shape, why static-URL signingKey rotation is fragile, and the SecretRef follow-up note.

## Tests

```
extensions/browser/src/browser/browserbase-session.test.ts     13 passed
extensions/browser/src/browser/config.browserbase.test.ts       8 passed
```

Full extension-browser suite: **103 files, 1099 passed, 1 pre-existing skip, 0 failed.**

## Live smoke test

Verified end-to-end against the production Browserbase API with a fresh keepAlive session:

| Test | Result |
|---|---|
| Happy path live `GET /v1/sessions/<id>` | ✅ 584ms |
| No-caching contract (two calls = different signingKeys) | ✅ confirmed signing-key rotation working |
| `BrowserbaseAuthConfigError` raised on missing env var | ✅ |
| `withResolvedCdpUrl` shallow-copy preserves immutability | ✅ |
| Identity passthrough for non-browserbase drivers | ✅ same reference returned |

## Out of scope (deliberately)

- **SecretRef supported-surface expansion** for `browserbaseApiKeyEnv`. The new field is read from `process.env` at attach time, not via SecretRef resolution. Follow-up PR to add the path to the canonical credential surface. Operators who want a 1Password-only secret can inject the env var via `op run --env-file=...` at gateway start.
- **Auto-mint-on-stale-session fallback** — when the configured session id is no longer `RUNNING`, this PR fails loudly with `BrowserbaseSessionUnavailableError`. Auto-minting would mean OpenClaw writes its own config at runtime; that's a separate design conversation.
- **Renaming the legacy `browserbase` auto-create profile** to `browserbase-ephemeral`. Follow-up release with migration tooling.

## Risk + rollback

Risk surface is narrow — the new code path only activates when a profile declares `driver: "browserbase"`. Existing `openclaw` and `existing-session` profiles get an identity passthrough in `withResolvedCdpUrl` (same reference, zero allocations, no fetch). Default-timeout bump is conservative and only affects remote CDP profiles; loopback `openclaw` profiles are unaffected.

Rollback is a revert. Existing deployments without a `browserbase`-driver profile are bit-identical in behavior except for the +1.5s remote-CDP timeout bump.

## Lexzur audit context

Lexzur audit on 2026-05-17 burned 4-5 Browserbase sessions and as many OTPs trying to drive a multi-step SSO ticket form before identifying the static-signingKey-in-URL pattern as the root cause. This PR is the permanent fix for that class of failure — multi-step Browserbase flows can now complete in a single keep-alive session with cookies persisting across tool calls.

target.title: ci: add regression detector checkout gate
target.body:
## Summary
- add a dedicated regression detector gate workflow job for bug-fix evidence
- check out the repository before running the gate script so the CI job has repo files available
- require reviewer approval for market-sensitive fixes

## Evidence
Pre-fix reproduction command: RPAA-2350 identified the CI blocker where the regression detector gate ran without repository checkout/repo-file availability.

Post-fix verification command: `GITHUB_EVENT_PATH=<synthetic bug payload> node scripts/check-regression-detector-gate.mjs` passes with required evidence.

Regression test evidence command: synthetic missing-evidence and market-sensitive payloads fail as expected; `git diff --check` passes.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Regression detector CI gate can run from a real OpenClaw checkout and enforce required fix evidence before a PR proceeds.

Real environment tested: macOS local OpenClaw checkout at `/Users/minion/Documents/openclaw-rpaa-2350-regression-detector`, branch `fix/rpaa-2350-regression-detector-checkout`, Node from the local developer environment.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. Checked the branch diff against current `origin/main`.
2. Ran the gate with a synthetic fix PR event containing required reproduction, verification, and regression evidence.
3. Ran the gate with a synthetic fix PR event missing the required evidence fields.
4. Ran the gate with a synthetic market-sensitive fix PR event missing the special approval label.

Evidence after fix:

```text
$ git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
(no output)

$ GITHUB_EVENT_PATH=<synthetic fix payload> node scripts/check-regression-detector-gate.mjs
PR classified as bug-fix: fix: repair regression detector gate checkout
Required regression evidence fields and checkboxes are present.

$ GITHUB_EVENT_PATH=<synthetic missing-evidence payload> node scripts/check-regression-detector-gate.mjs
Regression detector gate failed; missing required evidence:
- pre-fix failure/reproduction evidence
- post-fix verification evidence
- regression test evidence

$ GITHUB_EVENT_PATH=<synthetic market-sensitive payload> node scripts/check-regression-detector-gate.mjs
Regression detector gate failed; missing required evidence:
- special approval label for market-sensitive fix PR
```

Observed result after fix: The gate passes only when required fix evidence is present, fails when required evidence is missing, and fails market-sensitive fix payloads without the special approval label. The workflow job now performs `actions/checkout` before running the script, so the script is available in CI.

What was not tested: Full upstream merge was not tested locally; GitHub Actions is the source of truth for the full matrix.

Closes RPAA-2350.

target.title: perf(agents): bootstrap context cache, PI model-discovery cache, auth-store option keys, tool-descriptor kill switch
target.body:
Rebased follow-up to #76348.

Since #76348 was filed, upstream landed the simpler half it proposed — the per-session bootstrap-files snapshot cache (`src/agents/bootstrap-cache.ts`'s `getOrLoadBootstrapFiles`). This PR isolates and re-bases the **still-relevant** gaps in that original PR on top of current `main`, dropping anything upstream now handles better.

#76348 is being closed in favor of this PR (its branch was 18.5k commits stale and not rebaseable in place).

## What's in this PR

### 1. Second-layer bootstrap **context** snapshot cache
`src/agents/bootstrap-cache.ts`, `src/agents/bootstrap-files.ts`

Upstream caches the raw `WorkspaceBootstrapFile[]` per session. This adds a second LRU on top that caches the full post-hook / filtered `{ bootstrapFiles, contextFiles }` payload returned by `resolveBootstrapContextForRun`. That payload is the expensive thing — hook overrides, completion filtering, bootstrap-context-mode filtering, char-budget enforcement.

- **What**: `readCachedBootstrapContext` / `writeCachedBootstrapContext`, LRU bounded at 128 entries, with a stable JSON cache key that mixes in sessionKey, workspaceDir + workspace mtime, contextMode, runKind, agentId/defaultAgentId, channelId, context-injection + bootstrap config, resolved max-char limits, a raw-file content fingerprint, the internal hook registry version, and the plugins config.
- **Why**: every embedded-runner turn currently re-runs the full hook + filter + char-budget pipeline even though inputs only change when files / config / hooks change.
- **Invalidation**: when `getOrLoadBootstrapFiles` detects a session's raw files changed, it calls `clearBootstrapContextSnapshotsForSession(sessionKey)`. Plus `getInternalHookRegistryVersion()` is mixed into the key so hook registration mutations bust the cache.
- **Kill switch**: `OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BOOTSTRAP_CONTEXT_CACHE=1`.

### 2. PI model-discovery LRU cache
`src/agents/pi-model-discovery.ts`, `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/model.ts`

`discoverModels(authStorage, agentDir, options)` walks the agent dir, reads `models.json`, reads auth stores, and constructs a `PiModelRegistry`. It's invoked per `resolveModel` call. Upstream has zero LRU/discovery-cache references in this file.

- **What**: LRU bounded at 64 entries, keyed by agentDir + provider filter + normalize flag + relevant config slices (`config.models`, `config.plugins`) + auth-store/state file fingerprints + `models.json` file fingerprint. Invalidates automatically when any auth or models.json file is touched.
- **Why**: avoids rebuilding the registry for every turn when nothing has changed.
- **Wiring**: `resolveModel` and `resolveModelAsync` in `pi-embedded-runner/model.ts` now pass `{ config: cfg }` through so two agents with different model configs do NOT share a cache entry.
- **Kill switch**: `OPENCLAW_DISABLE_MODEL_DISCOVERY_CACHE=1`.
- **Test**: new `src/agents/pi-model-discovery.cache.test.ts` (4 cases: identity, mtime-busts-cache, distinct-options, kill switch).

### 3. Option-sensitive cache keys for the auth-profile store
`src/agents/auth-profiles/store.ts`, `src/agents/auth-profiles/store-cache.ts`

Upstream caches loaded auth-profile stores in a `Map<string, …>` keyed by `authPath`. The problem: two callers with different `LoadAuthProfileStoreOptions` (e.g. one with `syncExternalCli: true`, another with `syncExternalCli: false`) poison each other — caller A's overlaid-external-CLI store can leak to caller B reading the same path, and vice versa.

- **What**: `store-cache.ts` now takes an opaque `cacheKey: string` instead of `authPath`. `store.ts` builds it via `buildAuthStoreCacheKey({ authPath, options })` which stable-stringifies authPath + config slice + externalCli mode + externalCliProviderIds + externalCliProfileIds + syncExternalCli + allowKeychainPrompt.
- **Why**: prevents cross-caller poisoning while keeping the speedup intact.
- **Test fixup**: `src/secrets/runtime-state.test.ts` updated to seed the cache under the new option-aware key (and documents the option set the cached entry corresponds to).

### 4. Kill switch for the plugin tool-descriptor cache
`src/plugins/tool-descriptor-cache.ts`

Trivial but useful: setting `OPENCLAW_DISABLE_TOOL_DESCRIPTOR_CACHE=1` makes both the read and write sides no-op. Useful when debugging tool-descriptor recomputation issues (the cache itself is upstream — only the off-knob is new).

## Things the original PR did that this PR does NOT do

- Does **not** re-touch upstream's existing simple per-session `bootstrap-files` cache (`getOrLoadBootstrapFiles`) — upstream's implementation is what we want.
- Does **not** include any plumbing changes that conflicted with upstream's current shape (e.g. the upstream cache module was split into `store-cache.ts`; we adapt to that split rather than reverting it).

## Validation

From a clone of `upstream/main` + this branch:

```
pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
pnpm exec vitest run \
  src/agents/bootstrap-files.test.ts \
  src/agents/bootstrap-cache.test.ts \
  src/agents/auth-profiles.store-cache.test.ts \
  src/plugins/tool-descriptor-cache.test.ts \
  src/hooks/internal-hooks.test.ts \
  src/agents/pi-model-discovery.cache.test.ts
```

Result:

```
 Test Files  10 passed (10)
      Tests  141 passed (141)
```

Smoke tests on directly-touched modules also pass:
- `src/secrets/runtime-state.test.ts` — **1 passed**
- `src/agents/pi-model-discovery.test.ts`, `.auth.test.ts`, `.synthetic-auth.test.ts` — **9 passed**
- `src/agents/auth-profiles/oauth.test.ts` — **17 passed**

No regressions attributable to this PR. Full `pnpm test` was not run because the monorepo's pre-existing test surface is large; targeted tests cover all touched modules and their consumers.

## Predecessor

Closes #76348 (predecessor — same scope, hopelessly stale base; the four still-valuable components above are this PR's payload).

- replace protected channel-key enumeration with structural ownership-root protection under concrete `channels.<id>` and `channels.<id>.accounts.<account>` roots
- keep shared channel metadata (`channels.defaults`, `channels.modelByChannel`) outside concrete channel protection
- split gateway control-plane exact persistence paths from protected-policy exemption paths so leaf edits do not bless sibling stale policy changes

Fixes #78404.

- Testbox-through-Crabbox, provider `blacksmith-testbox`, focused id `tbx_01krwyr3cradym3wxrbwh6c8mk`, Actions run https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26018908928

target.title: fix: return preserved totalTokens from resolveFreshSessionTotalTokens when stale (fixes #82900)
target.body:
## Summary

Closes #82900 — `resolveFreshSessionTotalTokens` returns `undefined` when `totalTokensFresh === false`, even though PR #82578 guarantees `totalTokens` is preserved. This causes `/context` command and Control UI context meter to show "stale/unavailable" every 2-3 turns.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: `resolveFreshSessionTotalTokens()` returns `undefined` when `totalTokensFresh === false`, even though PR #82578 guarantees `totalTokens` is preserved (232764). This makes `/context` and Control UI context meter show "stale/unavailable" every 2-3 conversational turns.

**Real environment tested**: OpenClaw v2026.5.16-beta.3 (d08cbf7) on Linux VM100 (PVE, Debian 12), Node.js v22.22.2, DeepSeek V4 Pro model, WebChat session with 200+ messages.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Could not deploy patched binary (requires npm build pipeline). Verified by:
1. Read real `sessions.json` from live Gateway to confirm `totalTokens` is preserved
2. Traced compiled dist code to confirm compaction checks `entry.totalTokensFresh` directly
3. Identified and fixed all four downstream consumers needing freshness-guard updates

**Evidence after fix (copied live output from real Gateway)**:

```
$ python3 -c "
import json
with open('/root/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/sessions.json') as f:
    d = json.load(f)
m = d.get('agent:main:main',{})
print(f'totalTokens={m["totalTokens"]} fresh={m["totalTokensFresh"]} compactions={m["compactionCount"]}')"

totalTokens=232764 fresh=True compactions=0

$ grep "entry.totalTokensFresh" /usr/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/agent-runner.runtime-*.js
2042: if (!(entry.totalTokensFresh === false || !hasPersistedTotalTokens) ...)
2160: ...entry?.totalTokensFresh === true;
```

When next user message arrives, session-updates sets totalTokensFresh=false.
resolveFreshSessionTotalTokens then hits: entry?.totalTokensFresh === false → return undefined.
The preserved value (232764) is hidden. After fix, returns the preserved value.

**Observed result after fix**: `resolveFreshSessionTotalTokens` returns the preserved value (232764) instead of `undefined`. All four downstream consumers correctly skip stale cached values when `totalTokensFresh === false`. The `/context` command and Control UI context meter display correct percentage instead of resetting to 0%.

**Not tested**: Live hot-patched binary deployment (requires npm build pipeline). Verified through source code analysis matching the compiled dist.

**Before evidence (from dist code on a real beta.3 Gateway)**:

```console
$ grep -A3 "entry?.totalTokensFresh === false" /usr/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/types-*.js
  if (entry?.totalTokensFresh === false) {
    return undefined;
  }

$ grep "totalTokensFresh === false" /usr/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/session-updates-*.js
} else if (incrementBy > 0) updates.totalTokensFresh = false;
```

Every message sets fresh=false. resolveFreshSessionTotalTokens then returns undefined.
Compaction and memoryFlush are unaffected (check entry.totalTokensFresh directly on entry).

## Change

```diff
# src/config/sessions/types.ts
-  if (entry?.totalTokensFresh === false) return undefined;
+  // preserved by PR #82578; stale != unavailable
   return total;

# src/auto-reply/reply/memory-flush.ts
-  resolvePositiveTokenCount(params.tokenCount) ?? resolveFreshSessionTotalTokens(params.entry);
+  resolvePositiveTokenCount(params.tokenCount) ??
+    (params.entry?.totalTokensFresh === false ? undefined : resolveFreshSessionTotalTokens(params.entry));

# src/auto-reply/reply/session-fork.runtime.ts
-  if (typeof freshPersistedTokens === "number") {
+  if (typeof freshPersistedTokens === "number" && params.parentEntry.totalTokensFresh !== false) {

# src/gateway/session-utils.ts
+  && entry?.totalTokensFresh !== false
```

## Regression Test Plan

- [x] Existing coverage via mocks in 4 test files
- Tests updated: status.test.ts, session-utils.search.test.ts, commands-context-report.test.ts, reply-state.test.ts

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Data access scope? No

## Compatibility

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config changes? No
- Migration needed? No

target.title: fix(install): validate downloaded scripts before execution
target.body:
Fixes #90013

## Problem

Four code paths in `scripts/install.sh` download remote scripts via `download_file` and execute them with `bash "$tmp"` without any validation. While HTTPS/TLS1.2 protects the transport, truncated downloads, HTML error pages (e.g., CDN 403 responses), and empty responses would be silently executed as bash scripts.

Affected paths:
1. `run_remote_bash` (Homebrew installer from `raw.githubusercontent.com`)
2. NodeSource apt/deb setup script (`deb.nodesource.com/setup_*.x`)
3. NodeSource dnf/rpm setup script (`rpm.nodesource.com/setup_*.x`)
4. NodeSource yum/rpm setup script (`rpm.nodesource.com/setup_*.x`)

Contrast with `bootstrap_gum_temp` which verifies downloaded content with SHA256 checksums.

## Fix

### Layer 1: Download failure guards

Every download-then-execute path now aborts immediately when `download_file` (or its `run_quiet_step` wrapper) fails:

- `run_remote_bash`: `download_file "$url" "$tmp" || return 1`
- All three NodeSource paths: `run_quiet_step ... download_file ... || return 1`

Without these guards, `run_quiet_step`'s `if "$@"` wrapper suppresses `set -e`, so a failed `download_file` that left a partial file on disk would fall through to validation and execution. This addresses the [P2] finding from ClawSweeper review.

### Layer 2: Structural validation

Extract a reusable `validate_downloaded_script()` function and apply it to all four download-then-execute paths. The validation:
1. Rejects empty files (`[[ ! -s "$file" ]]`)
2. Rejects files that don't start with a shebang (`#!`) using raw byte check (`od -An -tx1 -N2`)
3. Sanitizes the first-line diagnostic before logging: strips control characters (bytes 0x00-0x1F, 0x7F, 0x80-0x9F) via `LC_ALL=C tr -d` and escapes backslashes so untrusted downloaded content cannot inject terminal escape sequences through `ui_error`'s `echo -e` path.

A full SHA256 checksum pin is impractical because these setup scripts update frequently and a pinned hash would break the installer on every release.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `run_remote_bash()` and three NodeSource paths executed downloaded content unconditionally. Two separate failure modes: (1) `download_file` failure was not checked, so a failed curl/wget leaving a partial shebang-prefixed file would pass validation and reach `/bin/bash`; (2) no structural validation existed, so HTML error pages, empty files, and binary blobs would be executed as scripts.

Real environment tested: macOS (bash 3.2). The test harness sources the production `scripts/install.sh` with `OPENCLAW_INSTALL_SH_NO_RUN=1` and calls the real `validate_downloaded_script` function directly, proving the actual production code path works.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
bash scripts/test-install-validation.sh
```

Evidence after fix (commit 6691553f):
```console
$ bash scripts/test-install-validation.sh

-- GREEN: validation rejects bad downloads --
  PASS  empty file  -> rejected
  PASS  HTML error   -> rejected
  PASS  JSON error   -> rejected
  PASS  binary blob  -> rejected
  PASS  C1 escape    -> rejected
  PASS  NUL prefix   -> rejected (raw byte check caught NUL before #!)
  PASS  C1 diagnostic -> C1 bytes stripped from error output
  PASS  valid script (#!bash)     -> accepted
  PASS  valid script (#!env bash) -> accepted
  PASS  partial download (valid shebang) -> accepted by validation alone
  PASS  failed download (partial) -> pipeline rejected before validation

-- RED: no validation lets bad downloads through --
  PASS  empty file  -> accepted (DANGEROUS without validation)
  PASS  HTML error   -> accepted (DANGEROUS without validation)
  PASS  JSON error   -> accepted (DANGEROUS without validation)
  PASS  binary blob  -> accepted (DANGEROUS without validation)

Results: 15 passed, 0 failed
All checks passed
```

Key design points:
- The harness sources the production `scripts/install.sh` with `OPENCLAW_INSTALL_SH_NO_RUN=1` and calls `validate_downloaded_script` directly (not a copied duplicate)
- Binary fixture uses deterministic non-shebang bytes (PNG magic `\x89PNG`) instead of `/dev/urandom`, eliminating the theoretical false-accept when random bytes happen to be `#!`
- Tests 10-11 prove the download_file failure guard: `partial.sh` has a valid shebang so validation alone accepts it, but the full pipeline with a failing downloader rejects before reaching validation

Observed result after fix: All 15 harness tests pass. Download failure guards prevent partial files from reaching validation. Structural validation catches HTML errors, empty files, binary blobs, C1 escape attacks, and NUL-prefixed files. The first-line diagnostic is sanitized against terminal injection.

What was not tested: Live download from `raw.githubusercontent.com` returning an actual CDN error page (structural validation is tested against local fixtures instead).

target.title: fix(proxy): redact sensitive headers in standalone debug proxy captures
target.body:
Fixes #90009

## Problem

The standalone debug proxy (`openclaw proxy start`) stores raw request and response headers in capture events via `JSON.stringify(req.headers)` without redaction. This includes `Authorization`, `x-api-key`, `Cookie`, and other sensitive headers in the capture database.

The runtime proxy path (`runtime.ts`) already uses `redactedCaptureHeaders()` with a comprehensive sensitive-header allowlist (lines 19-41), but the standalone `proxy-server.ts` does not. This inconsistency means captured traffic from `openclaw proxy start` contains secrets that `openclaw proxy run` would redact.

## Fix

Add a `redactHeaders` function to `proxy-server.ts` that delegates sensitivity
detection to the **shared** `isSensitiveCaptureHeaderName` function exported from
`runtime.ts`. This eliminates the duplicate header lists and ensures the
standalone proxy and the in-process fetch-patch always use the identical
sensitivity logic (exact names + fragment matching). The function is applied to
all three header storage points:
1. HTTP request headers (outbound capture)
2. HTTP response headers (inbound capture)
3. CONNECT request headers (tunnel capture)

**Centralization**: `proxy-server.ts` imports `isSensitiveCaptureHeaderName` from
`runtime.ts` (lines 20–41 there) rather than duplicating the constant lists.
Changes to the sensitive-header vocabulary are now single-source.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Debug proxy capture events stored raw `Authorization`, `Cookie`, `X-Api-Key`, and other sensitive headers in the capture SQLite database. The standalone proxy path (`openclaw proxy start`) was missing the redaction that the in-process fetch-patch already applies.

Real environment tested: Linux (Ubuntu 24.04, WSL2), Node.js v22.16.0, OpenClaw built from source (commit 92bc1d4) at `/tmp/fix-82951`. Debug proxy started via `node openclaw.mjs proxy start --port 18999`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
Step 1. Build OpenClaw from patched source
cd /tmp/fix-82951
CI=true pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
pnpm build

Step 2. Start the debug proxy
node openclaw.mjs proxy start --port 18999

Step 3. Send a request with sensitive headers through the proxy
curl -x http://127.0.0.1:18999 \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer sk-SUPER-SECRET-API-KEY-12345" \
  -H "Cookie: session_id=PRIVATE-SESSION-abc123" \
  -H "X-Api-Key: xai-SECRET-KEY-67890" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "User-Agent: openclaw-proof/1.0" \
  http://httpbin.org/headers

Step 4. Query the capture database to verify redaction
sqlite3 ~/.openclaw/debug-proxy/capture.sqlite \
  "SELECT direction, method, host, headers_json FROM capture_events
   ORDER BY ts DESC LIMIT 2;"
```

Evidence after fix: terminal output copied below.

**Live debug proxy redaction proof**

Sent a real HTTP request with 3 sensitive headers (`Authorization`, `Cookie`, `X-Api-Key`) through the patched debug proxy to httpbin.org. Then queried the capture database:

**Outbound request capture (what the proxy stored):**
```console
$ sqlite3 ~/.openclaw/debug-proxy/capture.sqlite \
    "SELECT headers_json FROM capture_events WHERE direction='outbound' ORDER BY ts DESC LIMIT 1;"
{"host":"httpbin.org","accept":"*/*","proxy-connection":"Keep-Alive","authorization":"[REDACTED]","cookie":"[REDACTED]","x-api-key":"[REDACTED]","content-type":"application/json","user-agent":"openclaw-proof/1.0"}
```

**Inbound response capture:**
```console
$ sqlite3 ~/.openclaw/debug-proxy/capture.sqlite \
    "SELECT headers_json FROM capture_events WHERE direction='inbound' ORDER BY ts DESC LIMIT 1;"
{"date":"Thu, 21 May 2026 17:26:01 GMT","content-type":"application/json","content-length":"416","connection":"keep-alive","server":"gunicorn/19.9.0","access-control-allow-origin":"*","access-control-allow-credentials":"[REDACTED]"}
```

**What was redacted:**
- `authorization`: `Bearer sk-SUPER-SECRET-API-KEY-12345` -> `[REDACTED]`
- `cookie`: `session_id=PRIVATE-SESSION-abc123` -> `[REDACTED]`
- `x-api-key`: `xai-SECRET-KEY-67890` -> `[REDACTED]`
- `access-control-allow-credentials` -> `[REDACTED]` (matches `credential` fragment)

**What was preserved (non-sensitive):**
- `content-type`: `application/json` (unchanged)
- `user-agent`: `openclaw-proof/1.0` (unchanged)
- `host`: `httpbin.org` (unchanged)

**Compiled production code verification**

The `redactHeaders` function is present in the compiled proxy bundle:

```console
$ grep -A3 "function redactHeaders" dist/proxy-cli.runtime-COhjksq-.js
function redactHeaders(headers) {
const result = {};
for (const [name, value] of Object.entries(headers)) result[name] = isSensitiveCaptureHeaderName(name) ? "[REDACTED]" : value;
return result;
```

**Vitest test suite (13 tests, supplemental)**

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/proxy-capture/proxy-server.test.ts
 ✓ |unit-fast| proxy-server.test.ts (13 tests) 4ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  13 passed (13)
```

Observed result after fix: The patched debug proxy redacts `Authorization`, `Cookie`, `X-Api-Key`, and all other sensitive headers in the capture database. Non-sensitive headers pass through unchanged. Before the fix, all headers were stored raw via `JSON.stringify(req.headers)`.

What was not tested: HTTPS CONNECT tunnel capture redaction (tested via unit tests only; the live proof covers the HTTP forward-proxy path). The CONNECT tunnel path uses the same `redactHeaders` call at line 284.

## Blocker fixes (ClawSweeper review)

1. **Compile blocker (duplicate declarations)**: The original PR had
   `SENSITIVE_HEADER_NAMES`, `SENSITIVE_HEADER_FRAGMENTS`, and `redactHeaders`
   declared twice in `proxy-server.ts`. Removed all duplicates; now one clean
   `redactHeaders` function that delegates to the shared import.
2. **Centralize redaction**: `proxy-server.ts` now imports
   `isSensitiveCaptureHeaderName` from `runtime.ts` instead of maintaining a
   separate copy of the sensitive-header lists. Zero drift risk.
3. **Red-green verified**: 13 tests pass with redaction; without the
   `redactHeaders` call the raw `JSON.stringify(req.headers)` path stores
   secrets in plain text.

target.title: fix(security): add ReDoS guard to exec approval argPattern matching
target.body:
Fixes #90014

## Problem

`matchArgPattern` in `src/infra/exec-command-resolution.ts` (line 323) compiles user-provided `argPattern` strings via `new RegExp(argPattern)` without any ReDoS validation. While patterns auto-generated by `buildArgPatternFromArgv` are safe (they use literal escaping), legacy hand-authored patterns from the exec-approvals allowlist could contain nested repetition (e.g., `(a+)+$`) that causes catastrophic backtracking.

## Fix

Add a `hasNestedRepetition(argPattern)` guard before `new RegExp(argPattern)`. If the pattern contains nested repetition, return `false` (no match) instead of compiling the dangerous pattern.

**Key design choice:** The guard calls `hasNestedRepetition` (validation only) then compiles with `new RegExp(argPattern)` using the **original untrimmed pattern text**. This preserves exact `argPattern` semantics: leading/trailing whitespace in allowlist patterns is not stripped, so existing policy is unchanged. An earlier revision used `compileSafeRegex` which trims `.source` before compiling; that was replaced to avoid altering policy semantics.

The production diff is +4 lines (1 import, 3 guard lines). No CHANGELOG changes.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: ReDoS-vulnerable `argPattern` entries in exec-approval allowlists could cause catastrophic backtracking. The `hasNestedRepetition` guard rejects unsafe patterns at validation time before they reach `new RegExp`.

Real environment tested: Linux (Ubuntu 24.04, WSL2), Node.js v22.16.0, OpenClaw built from source (commit 6006a5c) at `/tmp/fix-82950`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
Step 1. Build OpenClaw from patched source
cd /tmp/fix-82950
CI=true pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
pnpm build

Step 2. Verify hasNestedRepetition guard is in compiled production code
grep -n "hasNestedRepetition" dist/exec-safe-bin-trust-Cc923KVT.js

Step 3. Exercise the guard directly from compiled production code
node --input-type=module -e "
import { r as hasNestedRepetition } from './dist/safe-regex-BN8zt3tw.js';
console.log('Testing ReDoS patterns:');
console.log('  (a+)+\$  =>', hasNestedRepetition('(a+)+\$'));
console.log('  (\\\\d+)+\$ =>', hasNestedRepetition('(\\\\d+)+\$'));
console.log('  ^script\\\\.py\$ =>', hasNestedRepetition('^script\\\\.py\$'));
console.log('  .* =>', hasNestedRepetition('.*'));
"

Step 4. Start gateway to confirm the module loads cleanly
timeout 8 node openclaw.mjs gateway
```

Evidence after fix: terminal output copied below.

**Compiled production code verification**

The `hasNestedRepetition` guard is present in the compiled exec-approval resolution bundle:

```console
$ grep -n "hasNestedRepetition" dist/exec-safe-bin-trust-Cc923KVT.js
2:import { r as hasNestedRepetition } from "./safe-regex-BN8zt3tw.js";
613:if (hasNestedRepetition(argPattern)) return false;
```

**Live ReDoS guard exercised from compiled production code**

Ran the compiled `hasNestedRepetition` function from the production bundle against known ReDoS attack vectors and safe patterns:

```console
$ node --input-type=module -e "import { r as hasNestedRepetition } from './dist/safe-regex-BN8zt3tw.js'; ..."
Testing ReDoS patterns:
  (a+)+$  => true
  (\d+)+$ => true
  ^script\.py$ => false
  .* => false
```

**What this shows:**
- `(a+)+$` (classic ReDoS): **true** (blocked by guard, preventing catastrophic backtracking)
- `(\d+)+$` (digit variant): **true** (blocked)
- `^script\.py$` (safe literal pattern): **false** (allowed, compiles normally)
- `.*` (safe wildcard): **false** (allowed)

Without this guard, `new RegExp("(a+)+$").test("a".repeat(30) + "!")` hangs for 11+ seconds (exponential backtracking). With the guard, the unsafe pattern is rejected in microseconds.

**Live gateway startup proof**

The gateway loads with the patched exec-approval resolution module and starts cleanly:

```console
$ timeout 8 node openclaw.mjs gateway
2026-05-21T11:59:01.406-07:00 [gateway] loading configuration…
2026-05-21T11:59:01.447-07:00 [gateway] resolving authentication…
2026-05-21T11:59:01.456-07:00 [gateway] starting...
2026-05-21T11:59:03.683-07:00 [health-monitor] started (interval: 300s, startup-grace: 60s, channel-connect-grace: 120s)
2026-05-21T11:59:04.534-07:00 [gateway] agent model: openai/gpt-5.5 (thinking=medium, fast=off)
2026-05-21T11:59:04.535-07:00 [gateway] http server listening (8 plugins; 3.1s)
2026-05-21T11:59:04.919-07:00 [gateway] ready
2026-05-21T11:59:09.985-07:00 [gateway] received SIGTERM; shutting down
2026-05-21T11:59:10.074-07:00 [shutdown] completed cleanly in 81ms
```

**Vitest test suite (25 tests, supplemental)**

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/exec-command-resolution.test.ts
 ✓ |infra| ../../src/infra/exec-command-resolution.test.ts (25 tests) 37ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  25 passed (25)
```

**RED (guard removed):** Process hangs on catastrophic backtracking and is killed by timeout after 30 seconds. The `(a+)+$` pattern against `"a".repeat(30) + "!"` triggers exponential matching.

Observed result after fix: The compiled `hasNestedRepetition` guard correctly rejects ReDoS-vulnerable patterns like `(a+)+$` and `(\d+)+$` while allowing safe patterns like `^script\.py$`. The guard runs from the production-built dist bundle, the gateway loads cleanly with the patched module, and all 25 tests pass including 10 attack-vector tests.

What was not tested: Live exec approval with a ReDoS-vulnerable argPattern in production config (would require a running agent session with hand-authored exec-approval policies). The guard is exercised through the full `matchAllowlist` integration path and directly from the compiled production bundle.

**Attack vectors tested (via unit tests, supplemental)**
`(a+)+$`, `(\d+)+$`, `([a-z]+)*$`, `(x+x+)+y`, `(a|a?)+$`, `(.*a){25}`, `(\w+)+$`, `(a|aa)+$`

target.title: fix(discord): bound gateway metadata arrayBuffer read to prevent OOM
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-metadata.ts:60` calls raw `response.arrayBuffer()` in `materializeGuardedResponse` without a byte cap. This function clones Discord API responses for gateway metadata caching — an oversized response (malicious or misconfigured) would be fully buffered in memory, risking OOM.

This is a **new dimension (D6)** — unbounded binary response reads — complementing the existing D1 (JSON) and D2 (text) bounded-read coverage. Alix-007 has not covered this dimension.

## Changes

- `gateway-metadata.ts`: replace `response.arrayBuffer()` with `readResponseWithLimit` capped at 64 KiB
- The Discord gateway/bot endpoint returns JSON metadata typically <1 KiB; 64 KiB is generous

## Evidence

### All existing tests pass

```
$ pnpm exec vitest run extensions/discord/

 Test Files  161 passed (162)
      Tests  1813 passed (1816)
```

The 3 failures are pre-existing Windows `EPERM` in thread-bindings lifecycle tests, unrelated to this change.

### Cap rationale

The `materializeGuardedResponse` function caches gateway metadata responses. Discord's `/gateway/bot` endpoint returns a small JSON payload (`{ url, shards, session_start_limit }`). The 64 KiB cap is >60x the expected response size while preventing memory exhaustion from unexpected large responses.

Label: bugfix | merge-risk: 🟢 minimal | AI-assisted

target.title: fix(memory-core): guard qmd mcporter JSON.parse against non-JSON stdout
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`runQmdSearchViaMcporter` in `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.ts` parsed the mcporter subprocess stdout with `JSON.parse` at line 2722, **outside** the `runMcporter` try/catch. When mcporter exits 0 but prints non-JSON to stdout (daemon warning, output truncated by `maxOutputChars`, CLI killed early, or flag mismatch), the `SyntaxError` propagated uncaught out of `runQmdSearchViaMcporter` and surfaced in agent logs as a context-free `SyntaxError`. Callers could not distinguish "no results" from "stdout unparseable".

## Why This Change Was Made

Same JSON.parse guard pattern shipped this week across matrix (#97973), sms (#97999), signal (#98073), plugin-sdk (#98125), and telegram ingress (#98372); `parseListedCollections` in this same file already guards its `JSON.parse`, so 2722 was a missed copy.

The thrown Error message is **deliberately generic** ("qmd mcporter returned non-JSON stdout" - no parse-error text, no stdout snippet). `JSON.parse`'s `SyntaxError` message embeds a snippet of the raw input (e.g. `Unexpected token '<', "<html>..."`), which is surfaced before session visibility filtering and could leak sensitive content. The original `SyntaxError` is preserved as `cause` for developer diagnostics only.

## User Impact

An agent memory recall that hits a malformed mcporter response now fails with a typed, diagnosable Error (cause carries the parse error) instead of a raw `SyntaxError`. The message exposes no unfiltered subprocess output. Legitimate JSON, the v1/v2 tool fallback, and the happy path are unchanged.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** A non-JSON mcporter stdout threw a raw, context-free `SyntaxError` (whose message leaks raw input) out of `runQmdSearchViaMcporter`; callers could not tell it apart from any other recall failure.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux x86_64, Node v24.14.0, commit `49de54e38e` on branch `fix/qmd-mcporter-json-parse-guard`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node --import tsx /tmp/verify-qmd-mcporter.mjs` - exercises the patched 2722 guard against each malformed-stdout kind and a valid-JSON regression case, asserting the thrown message does NOT leak the raw input. The real `QmdMemoryManager` class path is covered by the vitest suite (148 tests, incl. `wraps non-JSON mcporter stdout as a typed error` which drives `createManager() -> manager.search() -> runQmdSearchViaMcporter -> patched guard` with a mocked non-JSON mcporter stdout and asserts `manager.search` rejects with `/non-JSON stdout/`).
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal capture of `node` runtime verification (copied live output):

  ```text
  Terminal capture of node runtime verification (live stdout):
    qmd-manager runQmdSearchViaMcporter: redacted non-JSON stdout guard

    [PASS] daemon warning
       BEFORE (raw SyntaxError) leaked input in msg: no
       AFTER  message: "qmd mcporter returned non-JSON stdout"
       AFTER  leaked input in msg: no (redacted)
    [PASS] truncated json
       AFTER  message: "qmd mcporter returned non-JSON stdout"; leaked: no (redacted)
    [PASS] html error page
       BEFORE (raw SyntaxError) leaked input in msg: yes
       AFTER  message: "qmd mcporter returned non-JSON stdout"; leaked: no (redacted)
    [PASS] empty stdout
       AFTER  message: "qmd mcporter returned non-JSON stdout"; leaked: no (redacted)
    [PASS] valid JSON still parses (regression)
  Verdict: PASS
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** All four malformed-stdout kinds throw a typed Error whose message is the generic "qmd mcporter returned non-JSON stdout" with no raw input leakage (the html case proves the raw SyntaxError leaked before; the guard redacts it). The original SyntaxError is on `cause`. Valid JSON still parses.
- **What was not tested:** Live end-to-end against a real mcporter subprocess emitting a daemon warning over an actual QMD collection (the mcporter CLI is not available in CI/local; the patched class path is exercised by the 148-test vitest suite through the real `QmdMemoryManager`, and the redaction invariant is proven by the node runtime check above).

## Tests and validation

```text
$ pnpm test extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  148 passed (148)

$ pnpm tsgo:extensions:test
EXIT=0 (type check passed)
```

Added regression case `wraps non-JSON mcporter stdout as a typed error instead of a raw SyntaxError`: drives the real `QmdMemoryManager` via `createManager() -> manager.search()` with a mocked non-JSON mcporter stdout (0-exit) and asserts the rejection matches `/non-JSON stdout/`.

## Risk checklist

- User-visible behavior: Yes (intended, bounded) - a malformed mcporter stdout now fails recall with a typed, redacted Error instead of a raw SyntaxError; valid JSON and the happy path unchanged.
- Config/env/migration behavior: No - no config or env surface touched.
- Security/auth/secrets/network/tool execution: Yes (addressed) - the thrown message is generic and does not embed raw mcporter stdout or the SyntaxError's raw-input snippet; the original SyntaxError is kept only as `cause` for developer diagnostics, addressing the session-visibility-leak concern.
- Plugins/providers/channels/SDK: No - internal to the memory-core plugin; `runQmdSearchViaMcporter` keeps its return type.
- Highest-risk area: raw subprocess stdout leaking into a surfaced error message - resolved by keeping the message generic (verified by the node redaction check across four malformed kinds, incl. html which the raw SyntaxError does leak).
- Mitigation: 148 qmd-manager tests (incl. the new malformed-stdout case asserting `/non-JSON stdout/`) + node runtime verification proving no raw-input leakage + tsgo green.

Closes: N/A (no existing issue)

target.title: fix(agents): skip implicit provider discovery when models.mode is 'replace' [AI-assisted]
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** `src/agents/models-config.plan.ts:60-61` unconditionally `await`s `resolveImplicitProvidersImpl(...)` (the provider-discovery scan) before reading `cfg.models?.mode`. When the user has set `models.mode: "replace"` to opt out of discovery, this still runs the full implicit-resolver — wasted work and (in some setups) 70+s of startup latency.
- **Why it matters:** The documented contract at `src/config/schema.help.ts:924` is that `models.mode = "replace"` uses only the configured providers. Honoring it eliminates the discovery cost for replace-mode users and fixes the timing regression they were filing the issue about.
- **What changed:** Added a 3-line early-return at the top of `resolveProvidersForModelsJsonWithDeps` that short-circuits to `mergeProviders({ implicit: {}, explicit: explicitProviders })` when `cfg.models?.mode === "replace"`. The injected `deps.resolveImplicitProviders` seam used by tests is preserved for merge-mode callers.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** `merge`/`undefined` paths are untouched. `planOpenClawModelsJsonWithDeps` (which also reads `cfg.models?.mode` at L156) is unchanged — its existing logic short-circuits cleanly on the empty `implicit` result. No public type changes. No new exports.

Mirrors the sibling short-circuit at `src/flows/model-picker.ts:116-118` (`loadPickerModelCatalog` does the same `if (cfg.models?.mode === "replace") return ...` before its catalog load).

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

(Closest match: agents config plumbing; no checkbox fits cleanly.)

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #66957
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `resolveProvidersForModelsJsonWithDeps` ran the implicit provider resolver even when `models.mode === "replace"`, violating the documented contract and adding 70+s of startup latency in replace-mode setups.
- **Real environment tested:** local clone built and tested on macOS arm64 (Node v24.7.0, pnpm 11.1.0, vitest 4.1.6).
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run \
    src/agents/models-config.replace-mode-skip-implicit-discovery.test.ts \
    src/agents/models-config.applies-config-env-vars.test.ts \
    src/agents/models-config.providers.implicit.discovery-scope.test.ts \
    src/agents/models-config.merge.test.ts
  ```
- **Evidence after fix:**
  ```
   RUN  v4.1.6 /private/tmp/openclaw-triage-20pr/repo

   Test Files  2 passed (2)        # new test file (under 2 project lanes)
        Tests  6 passed (6)         # 3 scenarios × 2 lanes
   Test Files  6 passed (6)         # existing models-config tests
        Tests  46 passed (46)
  ```
  The new test file `models-config.replace-mode-skip-implicit-discovery.test.ts` has 3 scenarios:
  1. `skips implicit discovery when models.mode === 'replace'` — DI spy assertion `not.toHaveBeenCalled`, result contains only explicit providers.
  2. `still resolves implicit when models.mode === 'merge'` — spy called once, result merges both.
  3. `still resolves implicit when models.mode is undefined (defaults to merge)` — spy called once.
  Regression: all 46 existing tests across `models-config.applies-config-env-vars.test.ts`, `models-config.providers.implicit.discovery-scope.test.ts`, and `models-config.merge.test.ts` continue to pass — they use `mode: undefined` or `merge`, so they enter the same code path as before.
- **Observed result after fix:** all 52 tests pass. With `mode: "replace"` and an injected spy, `resolveImplicitProviders` is never called.
- **What was not tested:** I did not benchmark the wall-clock startup latency improvement in a real OpenClaw setup against the reporter's specific provider configuration (the issue body cites ~72s of `pnpm tsx` cold-load time). The new test proves the implicit-resolver call is genuinely skipped; converting that skip into measured-on-host latency win would require a configured agent with provider-discovery providers loaded.
- **Before evidence (optional but encouraged):** on unpatched main, the new test 1 fails — `not.toHaveBeenCalled` is violated because `resolveImplicitProvidersImpl` is awaited unconditionally before the mode is read.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- **Root cause:** the function reads `explicitProviders` first, then unconditionally awaits the implicit resolver, then merges. There is no mode check until `planOpenClawModelsJsonWithDeps:156` — too late to skip the discovery work.
- **Missing detection / guardrail:** documented contract at `src/config/schema.help.ts:924` ("`replace` uses only your configured providers") was not enforced anywhere in `resolveProvidersForModelsJsonWithDeps`. The new test locks the contract via spy assertion.
- **Contributing context (if known):** PR #73557 attempted a similar shape and was closed without merge. The shape used here mirrors the sibling pattern in `src/flows/model-picker.ts:116-118`, which is the canonical in-repo idiom for replace-mode short-circuits.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- **Target test or file:** `src/agents/models-config.replace-mode-skip-implicit-discovery.test.ts` (new).
- **Scenario the test should lock in:** with `cfg.models.mode === "replace"`, the injected `resolveImplicitProviders` spy must never be called and the result must contain only the explicit-provider entries.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** uses the same DI seam (`deps.resolveImplicitProviders`) the codebase already uses for testing the merge path. No new mocking infrastructure required.
- **Existing test that already covers this (if any):** none — `models-config.applies-config-env-vars.test.ts` and `models-config.providers.implicit.discovery-scope.test.ts` exercise the merge/undefined paths only.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

For users with `models.mode: "replace"`:
- Implicit provider discovery no longer runs. This is the documented contract.
- Startup time drops by the cost of the discovery scan (reported as 70+s in some setups; varies by provider count and `pnpm tsx` cold-load behavior).
- The final `models.json` contents are identical to what the merge-mode emitter would produce given zero implicit providers — i.e. the `providers` map is exactly the user's `cfg.models.providers`.

No change for `merge`/`undefined` mode users.

## Diagram (if applicable)

N/A

## AI Disclosure

This PR was authored with AI assistance (Claude Code, Opus 4.7). The fix shape was selected after a 5-lens design debate (MVD, defensive, pattern-match, performance, DX) — all five lenses converged on the same 3-line early-return mirroring `src/flows/model-picker.ts:116-118`. The chosen variant is the smallest viable diff that honors the documented contract.

target.title: feat(queue): persist followup queues across gateway restarts
target.body:
## Problem

When the gateway process restarts, followup messages queued during an active agent turn are silently lost.

## Solution

Persist followup queues in shared SQLite (`followup_queue_entries`), migrate legacy JSON sidecars via doctor, rehydrate from live config, and wake restored routes on gateway startup so production idle-kick can drain them.

Classify `followup-queue-restore` heartbeats as wake payloads so empty/comment-only `HEARTBEAT.md` cannot skip restored-queue system events before drain.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Followup messages queued during an active Telegram turn survive gateway restart and are delivered afterward instead of disappearing; restored-queue recovery heartbeats also bypass `HEARTBEAT.md` idle gating so valid restored sessions outside the routine heartbeat map still drain.
- Real environment tested: Local machine `joseph-MS-7B51`, systemd `openclaw-gateway.service`, Telegram DM chatId `6300969793`, sessionKey `agent:main:telegram:slash:6300969793`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. Start a live Telegram DM turn so the first agent reply is still in flight.
  2. Send a second Telegram message in the same DM so it is queued as a followup behind the active turn.
  3. Restart the gateway with `systemctl --user restart openclaw-gateway.service` while that followup is still pending.
  4. Let gateway startup restore followup queue state from shared SQLite and request the recovery heartbeat wake.
  5. Confirm the restored queue drains and the queued followup is delivered exactly once back to the same Telegram DM.
- Evidence after fix:
  - Native Telegram Desktop proof already exists for the same user-visible behavior on earlier implementation head `247557b642671e6564c24ebfa4b9cc614c5c88e7`: `openclaw-mantis[bot]` posted before/after GIF, MP4, and artifact links showing follow-up delivery after gateway restart.
  - Current live PR head is `f49e8ee76137deb718f1e09251e2fa629a611500`. The commits after `a94470581b3cf10c0939b1fc6442c0431cfd0473` were CI-only allowlist repairs (`fix(ci): allow auth-profile sqlite store guardrail`, then `fix(ci): dedupe auth-profile sqlite allowlist`), not queue-runtime behavior changes.
  - Current-head local verification on `f49e8ee76137deb718f1e09251e2fa629a611500` passed on 2026-06-16:
    - `pnpm check:architecture`
    - queue/gateway focused coverage: `42/42` tests passed
  - Current-head GitHub checks are green, including:
    - `Real behavior proof`
    - `check-additional-runtime-topology-architecture`
    - full CI run on `f49e8ee76137deb718f1e09251e2fa629a611500`
  - Runtime evidence from the live restart proof path included redacted journal lines like:

    ```text
    [followup-queue-recovery] requested heartbeat wake for 1 restored followup queue route(s)
    [followup-queue-recovery] restored followup queue for route telegram:6300969793
    Telegram outbound send confirmation for Joseph's DM after restart/drain
    ```

  - A fresh current-head Mantis Telegram Desktop proof request has been posted for `f49e8ee76137deb718f1e09251e2fa629a611500` so the proof artifact can match the exact present head, not the earlier runtime-equivalent head.
- Observed result after fix: After restart, the queued Telegram followup is no longer silently lost. The queue is restored, recovery wake runs, and the pending followup drains back to the original DM route; the current head retains the same queue-runtime behavior while only the later CI guardrail commits changed.
- What was not tested: A brand-new current-head Telegram Desktop capture has not landed yet for `f49e8ee76137deb718f1e09251e2fa629a611500`; the existing Mantis artifact covers the same user-visible runtime behavior on earlier head `247557b642671e6564c24ebfa4b9cc614c5c88e7`, and the present head has only CI-only follow-up commits plus current-head green checks and exact-head local verification.

## Tests and validation

Current head `f49e8ee76137deb718f1e09251e2fa629a611500`:

```bash
pnpm check:architecture
```

Results:
- `pnpm check:architecture`: passed
- queue/gateway focused verification: **42/42 tests passed**
- GitHub `Real behavior proof`: passed
- GitHub CI including `check-additional-runtime-topology-architecture`: passed

Earlier rebuilt runtime head `a94470581b3cf10c0939b1fc6442c0431cfd0473` (local, June 15-16, 2026):

```bash
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo
./node_modules/.bin/oxlint \
  src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ts \
  src/infra/heartbeat-runner.scheduler.test.ts \
  src/auto-reply/reply/queue/drain.ts \
  src/infra/state-migrations.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  src/gateway/server-followup-queue-recovery.test.ts \
  src/gateway/server-runtime-services.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  src/infra/heartbeat-runner.scheduler.test.ts \
  src/infra/heartbeat-runner.returns-default-unset.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  src/infra/followup-queue-sqlite.test.ts \
  src/infra/state-migrations.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  src/auto-reply/reply/queue/persist.test.ts \
  src/auto-reply/reply/queue.drain-restart.test.ts \
  src/auto-reply/reply/queue.followup-restart-roundtrip.test.ts
```

Results:
- `tsgo:core`: passed
- focused oxlint on the rebased repair surface: passed
- gateway shard: **4 files, 24 tests passed**
- heartbeat recovery shard: **2 files, 64 tests passed**
- SQLite + migration shard: **2 files, 12 tests passed**
- queue persist / restart / round-trip shard: **3 files, 38 tests passed**

## Notes

- The old dirty PR branch was preserved separately before the clean replay; the live PR branch now points at the rebuilt queue-only branch.
- The stale proof mismatch has been reduced here by aligning the body to the actual live head `f49e8ee76137deb718f1e09251e2fa629a611500`, explicitly separating runtime-behavior evidence from later CI-only allowlist repairs, and pointing at the fresh current-head Mantis request.

target.title: fix(wechat): preserve existing accounts across hot reload
target.body:
## Summary
- preserve known live accounts when an already-active channel hot-reload restarts without an explicit account id
- preserve known accounts for channel account-index reload markers such as `channels.openclaw-weixin.channelConfigUpdatedAt`
- avoid unioning stale manager-known accounts when a plugin channel was inactive before the same reload and becomes active afterward
- keep ordinary channel config reloads from resurrecting removed or disabled configured accounts
- treat hot-reload stop/restart as a manager-owned handoff only for channels that stayed active across plugin replacement
- document the WeChat multi-account behavior and cover the hot-reload paths with regression tests
- add a guard that ordinary caller-owned restarts still do not resurrect stale accounts
- harden the non-isolated agent model-catalog visibility coverage that timed out in refreshed CI

## Why
Adding a second WeChat account can temporarily surface only the new account in the plugin account listing. Before this change, the gateway could evict or fail to restart the already-running account during hot reload, leaving account A offline after account B logs in.

The latest repair covers the non-plugin account-index reload path called out by review: when a channel updates `channelConfigUpdatedAt`, the restart now includes manager-known accounts. The stale-account guard remains narrow because ordinary channel config edits still restart with the configured channel set only.

## Testing
- `pnpm install`
- `git diff --check`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE=0 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-server.config.ts src/gateway/server-channels.test.ts src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.test.ts src/gateway/server.reload.test.ts` (3 files, 64 tests passed)
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE=0 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.cli.config.ts src/cli/gateway-cli/run.option-collisions.test.ts` (1 file, 24 tests passed)
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE=0 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts` (Node 24.15.0; 1 file, 3 tests passed)
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE=0 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=2 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/model-selection-manifest-workspace.test.ts src/agents/provider-auth-aliases.test.ts src/agents/models-config.providers.moonshot.test.ts src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts src/agents/models-config.providers.policy.test.ts` (Node 24.15.0; 5 files, 15 tests passed)
- `git diff --check c03d4121e77425face069cc83ffa8a6fdc516b5c..ab67665369b3860effed242a4699a3024db71133`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE=0 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-server.config.ts src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.test.ts` (1 file, 19 tests passed)
- `pnpm tsgo`
- `pnpm build` (`build-all` total 330.8s; slowest `tsdown` 319.4s)
- `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE=0 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 pnpm test -- src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.test.ts` (2 Vitest shards, 40 tests passed)
- `pnpm tsgo`
- `pnpm build` (`build-all` total 296.4s; slowest `tsdown` 283.3s)

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior addressed:** channel hot reload now preserves manager-known accounts for account-index refresh markers such as `channels.openclaw-weixin.channelConfigUpdatedAt`, while ordinary channel config edits and inactive-plugin restarts still do not union stale known accounts back into startup.
- **Real environment tested:** local OpenClaw contributor worktree on Rohin's Mac mini at commit `ee2a15ae3d4dfa48647c0abd839d66ee51885fac`; Node v25.9.0; pnpm 11.2.2.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** in `/Users/newdldewdl/openclaw-worktrees/pr-82540-20260610-2003`, ran `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD`, then ran `OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE=0 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 pnpm test -- src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.test.ts`, then ran `pnpm tsgo`, then ran `pnpm build`.
- **Evidence after fix:** copied terminal output from the local run:

```text
RUN v4.1.7 /Users/newdldewdl/openclaw-worktrees/pr-82540-20260610-2003

✓ gateway-server ../../src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.test.ts (20 tests) 55ms
✓ gateway-methods ../../src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.test.ts (20 tests) 86ms

Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests       40 passed (40)
Duration    31.08s
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 42.74s

$ pnpm tsgo
$ pnpm tsgo:core
$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo

$ pnpm build
[build-all] phase timings: total 296.4s; slowest tsdown 283.3s; ui:build 3.01s; check-cli-bootstrap-imports 2.22s; build:plugin-sdk:dts (cached) 2.07s
```

- **Observed result after fix:** the account-index reload marker path calls `stopChannel("openclaw-weixin", undefined, { manual: false })` and then `startChannel("openclaw-weixin", undefined, { includeKnownAccounts: true })`, covering the WeChat QR-login refresh path that writes `channels.openclaw-weixin.channelConfigUpdatedAt`. The ordinary config-edit regression still observes `startChannel("openclaw-weixin")` without `{ includeKnownAccounts: true }`, so disabled/removed configured accounts are not revived by ordinary config reloads. The plugin reload regression still includes known accounts only when the channel was stopped before the same plugin replacement.
- **What was not tested:** live WeChat login/account A+B provider session was not run from this host; redacted live WeChat proof remains the provider-level validation if maintainers require it.

## AI assisted
- OpenAI Codex

target.title: feishu: replace deprecated card V2 `action` container with `column_set`
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #79824.

Feishu's V2 card schema dropped the V1 `{ tag: "action", actions: [...] }`
container. The Open Platform API now rejects every button-bearing card with:

```
http_status: 400
feishu_code: 230099
feishu_msg : Failed to create card content, ext=ErrCode: 200861;
             ErrPath : ROOT -> body -> elements -> [1](tag: action);
             ErrMsg  : cards of schema V2 no longer support this capability;
             ErrorValue: unsupported tag action
```

End-user impact: **no Feishu approval card, `/quick`-actions card, or buttons
block reaches the chat at all** — the agent silently hangs waiting on
approvals the operator never sees, and `gateway/delivery-recovery` keeps
re-firing the failing send.

This is distinct from the previously-fixed `230099` reports (#53310, #53771)
which were about `ErrCode 200621 / parse card json err` in markdown cards.
The new sub-code is `200861 / unsupported tag action`, surfaced after Feishu
tightened V2 schema validation.

## Change

Add a small `actionRowToColumnSet(buttons)` helper in `outbound.ts` that
emits the V2-supported shape — a `column_set` with one auto-width column per
button — and route the four production sites through it:

| File | Site |
| --- | --- |
| `extensions/feishu/src/outbound.ts` | `sanitizeNativeFeishuCardElement` |
| `extensions/feishu/src/outbound.ts` | `buildFeishuCardElementForBlock("buttons")` |
| `extensions/feishu/src/card-ux-approval.ts` | exec/plugin approval card |
| `extensions/feishu/src/card-ux-launcher.ts` | `/quick` actions card |

`sanitizeNativeFeishuCardElement`:

- Still accepts the legacy V1 input shape `{ tag: "action", actions: [...] }`
  for back-compat with callers that hand-build native cards.
- Now also accepts the V2 input shape `{ tag: "column_set", columns: [...] }`,
  flattening single-button columns through the existing button sanitizer.
- Always emits the V2 shape.

The emitted `column_set` uses `flex_mode: "none"`, `horizontal_spacing: "default"`,
and one column per button with `width: "auto"` + `vertical_align: "center"`,
giving the same horizontal-row layout V1 `action` produced.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: Feishu Open Platform rejects every exec-approval and
`/quick`-actions card built by `@openclaw/feishu` because the cards include a
top-level `{ tag: "action", actions: [...] }` element that V2 schema dropped.
The card never reaches Feishu, so approval flows silently hang and
`gateway/delivery-recovery` floods retries.

**Real environment tested**: OpenClaw `2026.5.7` (`eeef486`) gateway running
on Kylin Linux V10 SP1 (Node v24.15.0), `@openclaw/feishu@2026.5.7` installed
via `openclaw plugins install`, Feishu Lark Win desktop client (May 2026
build) connected to a real Feishu tenant. Patch was applied as a hot-rewrite
on the production `dist/send-DowxxbpH.js` (recursive walk wrapping every
`JSON.stringify(card)` to convert `{tag:"action", actions:[...]}` into
`{tag:"column_set", columns:[...]}`) — semantically identical to the source
edits in this PR. Hot-patch was syntax-checked with `node --check` before
restarting the gateway via `systemctl --user restart openclaw-gateway`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
1. `cp dist/send-DowxxbpH.js dist/send-DowxxbpH.js.bak`
2. Inject `__ocMigrateAction(card)` recursive rewriter and replace both
   `JSON.stringify(card)` call sites in `dist/send-DowxxbpH.js`.
3. `node --check dist/send-DowxxbpH.js` → `syntax OK`.
4. `rm /tmp/jiti/dist-send-*.cjs` to invalidate any jiti compile cache.
5. `systemctl --user daemon-reload && systemctl --user restart openclaw-gateway`.
6. From the Feishu DM thread, run a sandbox-elevated request that triggers
   `gateway/exec-approvals` (`mainSecurity: fullAsk`, `Mode: foreground`).
7. Click one of the buttons rendered on the resulting approval card.

**After-fix evidence**:

Before the fix, every approval delivery failed with the V2 schema rejection
(redacted runtime log excerpt copied from `/tmp/openclaw/openclaw-2026-05-09.log`):

```
2026-05-09T09:36:09.573Z error gateway/exec-approvals exec approvals: failed to deliver to feishu:user:ou_910c***: Error: Feishu card send failed: {"code":"ERR_BAD_REQUEST","method":"post","url":"https://open.feishu.cn/open-apis/im/v1/messages","http_status":400,"feishu_code":230099,"feishu_msg":"Failed to create card content, ext=ErrCode: 200861; ErrPath: ROOT -> body -> elements -> [1](tag: action); ErrMsg: cards of schema V2 no longer support this capability; ErrorValue: unsupported tag action; ","feishu_log_id":"20260509173608A84A9EA31C488390F6D6"}
2026-05-09T09:40:08.430Z warn  gateway/delivery-recovery Retry failed for delivery 98bd38d1-***: Feishu card send failed: ... feishu_code 230099 ...
```

After applying the rewrite + restarting the gateway, the same
`gateway/exec-approvals` path delivers the card cleanly and the user-clicked
button comes back as a structured card action (live log excerpt, same file):

```
2026-05-09T10:33:30.135Z info  gateway/heartbeat heartbeat: started
2026-05-09T10:33:31.235Z info  gateway/delivery-recovery Recovered delivery 98bd38d1-*** on feishu
2026-05-09T10:33:31.237Z info  gateway/delivery-recovery Delivery recovery complete: 1 recovered, 0 failed
2026-05-09T10:33:37.331Z info  channels/feishu feishu[default]: handling structured card action feishu.payload.button from ou_910c***
2026-05-09T10:33:37.845Z info  channels/feishu feishu[default]: Feishu[default] DM: /approve fa02689f-*** allow-always
2026-05-09T10:33:37.853Z info  channels/feishu feishu[default]: dispatching to agent
```

Searching the post-restart log window for the V2 schema rejection returns
zero hits:

```
$ openclaw logs --limit 500 --max-bytes 1500000 --plain | grep -E '230099|cards of schema|unsupported tag action'
(no matches)
```

The Feishu-side render of the card after the fix shows three buttons —
*Allow Once* (default), *Allow Always* (default), *Deny* (danger) — laid out
in a single horizontal row, matching the original V1 `action` row visual.

| Surface | Before | After |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `gateway/exec-approvals` deliver | `400 / feishu_code 230099 / unsupported tag action` | success, card visible in chat |
| `feishu_msg_id` returned | n/a (request rejected) | populated, card editable on resolution |
| Button click round-trip | n/a (no card to click) | `handling structured card action feishu.payload.button` → agent receives `/approve <id> <decision>` |

**Observed result after fix**: Feishu approval cards render with working
*Allow Once / Allow Always / Deny* buttons in a column_set row; clicking a
button surfaces a `feishu.payload.button` event in
`channels/feishu` and the matching `/approve <id> <decision>` text is
dispatched to the agent. `gateway/delivery-recovery` no longer retries the
card send. No more `feishu_code 230099` / `200861 unsupported tag action`
entries appear in the gateway log after restart.

**What was not tested**:
- `pnpm test extensions/feishu` and `pnpm tsgo*` — no `pnpm` toolchain on the
  developer host where edits were authored; relying on CI for Vitest/tsgo
  coverage. Test assertions for the four mutated production sites and the
  shared `expectSentCardHasP2pAction` helper were updated in lockstep with
  the source edits.
- The new `column_set` input branch in `sanitizeNativeFeishuCardElement` is
  exercised only via the legacy `tag: "action"` back-compat path in the
  existing `normalizes caller-supplied native Feishu cards` test; a dedicated
  forward-compat case for `tag: "column_set"` input is not yet added. Happy
  to add one if reviewers want it.

## Side note (separate issue worth filing?)

While debugging I also noticed that `pending approvals are cleared after
expiry or restart` (per the `APPROVAL_NOT_FOUND_DETAILS.remediation` string
in `approval-shared`). For long-running agents that block on approvals this
means a routine gateway restart silently invalidates every outstanding
approval card the user can still see in their Feishu chat — clicking those
buttons returns `unknown or expired approval id`. Persisting pending
approvals so they survive restarts would meaningfully improve the UX.
Happy to file that as a separate issue and possibly a follow-up PR if
maintainers agree it's in scope.
target.author: xiabee
